steps.title: Substitute the value x = 2
steps.content: Replace $x$ with $2$ in $$f(x) = x^3 + 15.$$ This gives $$f(2) = (2)^3 + 15.$$

steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(2) = 8 + 15 = 23.$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. The value of $f\left(2\right)$ is the value of $f\left(x\right)$ when $x=2$. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in the given function yields $f\left(2\right)={\left(2\right)}^{3}+15$, or $f\left(2\right)=8+15$, which is equivalent to $f\left(2\right)=23$. Therefore, the value of $f\left(2\right)$ is $23$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(2\right)$ when $f\left(x\right)=x\left(3\right)+15$, rather than $f\left(x\right)={x}^{3}+15$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
